messages.content: me and jordan broke up last weekend and i'm just kinda sad about it

I'm really sorry to hear that. Breakups are rough, especially when it's still so fresh - a week isn't very long at all.

Do you want to talk about it? Like what's going on in your head tonight, or just how you're feeling in general?
it was pretty mutual, nobody did anything terrible, we just wanted different things

Those can almost be harder in a weird way, you know? Like there's no one to be angry at, no clear villain - just this sad situation where two people who probably care about each other realize it's not going to work. That can leave you feeling kind of... lost, maybe?

How are you holding up day to day? Like are you doing okay at work/school and stuff, or is it hitting you pretty hard?
still though, three years. i keep reaching for my phone to text them stuff and then remembering

Oh, that habit is one of the hardest parts. Three years means Jordan was just *woven into* your daily life - of course your instincts are still reaching for that connection. It's going to take a while for that to catch up with reality, and every time it happens it's like a little reminder all over again.

What kind of stuff do you find yourself wanting to text them about?
i had a little cry tonight watching one of our old shows. felt good actually, like letting it out

That's actually really healthy - letting yourself feel it rather than pushing it down. And there's something kind of bittersweet and almost comforting about crying over something that meant something real, you know? It's like your heart processing things in its own way.

It's good that you're not just numbing out or trying to pretend you're fine. Three years is worth some tears.

Are you doing anything to take care of yourself tonight, or just kind of sitting with it?
i'll be ok, i know i will. just gonna be a sad few weeks and that's normal i think

Yeah, that's exactly right. You've got a really healthy perspective on it - not catastrophizing, just acknowledging that it's going to hurt for a bit and that's okay. That kind of self-awareness will carry you through it.

And it sounds like you know yourself pretty well. Letting yourself cry, giving yourself permission to be sad without panicking about it... that's good stuff.

Is there people around you - friends, family - who know what's going on and can check in on you?
